claims.claim: throughput_greedy**: Maximizes throughput regardless of pressure.

evidence_refs.quote: Evaluating LLM serving systems under memory pressure presents a gap between what quality-centric benchmarks measure and what deployment environments experience. Standard evaluation slices rank policies by output quality or throughput, but under unified memory architecture (UMA) pressure—where GPU and CPU share memory—serving policies that score well on quality may fail catastrophically via OOM aborts or long-context truncation.
